novel_features.name: Sold-comp intelligence
novel_features.description: Average sale price for any item over the last 90 days with smart matching, condition normalization, outlier trim, and percentile distribution.
novel_features.rationale: Marketplace Insights API is partners-only; non-partner developers have no programmatic path to 90-day sold history without HTML scraping. This combines the scrape with statistics nobody else surfaces.
novel_features.example: ebay-pp-cli comp "Cooper Flagg /50 Topps Chrome" --trim --json --select mean,median,sample_size
novel_features.why_it_matters: When pricing a bid, you need the realistic distribution of recent sales, not a single anchor. Trim handles outliers; dedupe handles title variants.

novel_features.name: True sniper bidding
novel_features.description: Hold a max bid client-side, fire through the user's authenticated session at lead-seconds before close. Other bidders never see the max until it's too late to react.
novel_features.rationale: eBay's Offer API (programmatic bidding) is partners-only. Web-based snipers like Gixen replay the same /bfl/placebid flow we captured during browser-sniff; this is the OSS CLI version.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reveals max only at fire time, not on bid-form open. Other bidders cannot react inside 8 seconds.

novel_features.name: Has-bids + ending-window auction discovery
novel_features.description: Search active auctions filtered by bid count and ending window (e.g. "Steph Curry cards with at least 3 bids ending in next hour").
novel_features.rationale: Browse API lost the bidCount filter when Finding API was retired in February 2025. HTML scrape of /sch/i.html with local post-filter is the replacement.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Finds price-discoverable competition windows where last-second bidding actually moves the price.

novel_features.name: Outlier-trimmed comp distribution
novel_features.description: 1.5x IQR outlier trim on sold-comp results. Surfaces the realistic price band buyers should anchor on, with stddev and quartiles.
novel_features.rationale: Single-listing 'last sold price' is a noisy anchor; mean alone hides distribution. Stats-grade trimming is the same approach driscoll42's ebayMarketAnalyzer uses, applied as a CLI flag.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Tells you what a normal buyer actually paid versus a record sale or a fire-sale outlier.

novel_features.name: Variant deduplication
novel_features.description: Collapse near-duplicate sold listings to one exemplar per fingerprint (token-bag, order-insensitive).
novel_features.rationale: Sold listings repeat the same item with shuffled title order, prefixed years, and condition tags. Order-insensitive fingerprinting handles "Cooper Flagg /50 Topps Chrome" matching "2025-26 Topps Chrome Cooper Flagg #251 Gold /50".
novel_features.why_it_matters: Without dedupe, the comp distribution is biased toward whichever seller listed the same card 5 times.

narrative.headline: Every eBay buyer feature a power user wants, plus a local sold-comp brain and a sniper that doesn't tell eBay your max.
narrative.value_prop: Buyer-power-user CLI for eBay. Sold-comp intelligence over 90 days, true sniper bidding (max held client-side), and has-bids+ending-window auction discovery in a single Go binary. No Marketplace Insights or Offer API access required.
narrative.auth_narrative: eBay's website uses cookie-based session auth with per-action signed-request-tokens (srt) and a per-bid Forter fraud fingerprint. `ebay-pp-cli auth login --chrome` imports your logged-in cookies; the CLI extracts srt and forterToken from each bid module page at fire time. No App ID required for the buyer surface.

quickstart.comment: Import cookies from your logged-in Chrome session
Verify the cookies and base reachability

quickstart.command: ebay-pp-cli comp "Cooper Flagg gold /50 Topps Chrome" --trim
quickstart.comment: Look up the 90-day sold distribution for a specific item
Dry-run a sniper plan before placing

troubleshoots.symptom: HTTP 403 Access Denied / Akamai challenge on every command
troubleshoots.fix: Run `ebay-pp-cli auth login --chrome` to import a fresh cookie set; Akamai relaxes throttling for sessions tied to a logged-in user.

troubleshoots.symptom: snipe returns 'could not extract srt token from bid module'
troubleshoots.fix: Cookies have expired. Re-run `auth login --chrome` and retry within a few minutes.

troubleshoots.symptom: comp returns 0 sample_size for a query that obviously has sold listings
troubleshoots.fix: You're being throttled. Wait 60s and retry; or run `ebay-pp-cli auth login --chrome` first to clear the challenge.

narrative.when_to_use: When you need to know what something has actually sold for on eBay over the last 90 days, you want to bid on an auction without revealing your max to the proxy bidder, or you need to discover competitive auctions filtered by bid count and ending time. Domain-agnostic: works for cards, watches, electronics, vintage, anything sold on eBay. Not for sellers — this is the buyer-power-user CLI.

recipes.title: Comp before bidding
recipes.explanation: Fetches and analyzes 90 days of sold listings; outputs only the distribution stats.

recipes.title: Find under-priced auctions ending soon
recipes.explanation: Active auctions with at least one bid, ending within two hours, under $5K. Pipe to jq for further filtering.

recipes.title: Cross-condition sold-comp comparison
recipes.command: ebay-pp-cli comp "Zion Williamson rookie" --condition graded --trim --json --select mean,median
recipes.explanation: Filter sold comps to graded copies only; compare against `--condition raw` to see the grading premium.

recipes.title: Simulate a snipe before placing
recipes.explanation: Returns the planned bid amount, the bid URL, and a sanity-check note without firing the trisk + confirmbid flow.

recipes.title: Bulk comp lookup from CSV
recipes.command: cat queries.txt | xargs -I{} ebay-pp-cli comp {} --trim --json --agent --select query,mean,median
recipes.explanation: Pipe a list of search strings; emits one JSON line per item for spreadsheet import.

trigger_phrases: what did this sell for on eBay
show me cards with bids ending soon
